key_features: Hilt dependency injection setup with version 2.48
Comprehensive testing dependencies: JUnit, Mockito, Turbine, Espresso
file_insights.significance: Core configuration file for the app module

file_insights.purpose: ProGuard obfuscation rules for release builds
key_features: Preserves annotations and metadata required by Hilt
file_insights.significance: Ensures Hilt dependency injection works in obfuscated builds

key_features: Bash permissions for Python tools and Claude MCP
Added Windows-specific JBR (Java Runtime) path for Android Studio
file_insights.significance: Ensures proper Java 17 runtime for build consistency

patterns_discovered.description: Using Jetpack Compose, Kotlin Coroutines, Hilt DI, and Material3
patterns_discovered.implication: Project targets contemporary Android development practices

patterns_discovered.description: KAPT with correctErrorTypes enabled and worker API
patterns_discovered.implication: Optimized for incremental compilation and Hilt code generation

patterns_discovered.description: Includes unit tests (JUnit, Mockito), integration tests (Espresso), and flow testing (Turbine)
patterns_discovered.implication: Project structure supports TDD and quality assurance practices
Possible Gradle version migration or multiple build environments

patterns_discovered.description: ProGuard rules specifically preserve Hilt, Coroutines, and scanner components

gotchas_discovered.description: CompileOptions and kotlinOptions both target Java 17, minSdk 24 may have compatibility concerns
gotchas_discovered.risk: Runtime failures on older Android versions if string APIs from Java 17 are used

gotchas_discovered.description: gradle.properties contains Windows-specific absolute path (C:\Program Files\Android\Android Studio\jbr)
gotchas_discovered.risk: Build will fail on macOS/Linux unless path is adjusted; should use environment-relative path

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Gradle cache files in version control
gotchas_discovered.description: Multiple .gradle directories and binary cache files added to repository
gotchas_discovered.risk: Repository bloat, merge conflicts, and machine-specific cache pollution
Version mismatch between Kotlin plugin and Compose compiler can cause cryptic errors

gotchas_discovered.description: Multiple interconnected ProGuard rules needed for Hilt, BroadcastReceiver, and data classes
gotchas_discovered.risk: Incomplete rules can cause runtime reflection failures in release builds

approach_outcome.summary: Successfully created a fully-configured app module with Hilt dependency injection, modern Jetpack Compose UI framework, and comprehensive testing infrastructure
achievements: Established clean build.gradle.kts with proper Hilt 2.48 setup and KAPT configuration
Integrated Jetpack Compose with Material3 design system
Added comprehensive test dependencies and frameworks
Created ProGuard rules protecting Hilt-generated and reflection-dependent code
Set up Kotlin 17 with compatible compiler extension
approach_outcome.completeness: Module is ready for feature development with proper DI, UI, and testing foundations

recommendations.recommendation: Add .gradle/ to .gitignore to prevent caching generated files
recommendations.rationale: Binary cache files should not be version controlled; they are machine and version-specific

recommendations.recommendation: Replace absolute JBR path in gradle.properties with environment variable or relative reference
recommendations.rationale: Current Windows path breaks builds on macOS/Linux; use %ANDROID_HOME% or ./jbr approach

recommendations.recommendation: Document Java 17 requirement and minSdk 24 compatibility implications
recommendations.rationale: Java 17 features may not be available on all minSdk 24 devices; clarify compatibility strategy

recommendations.recommendation: Consider using Gradle build cache and local build cache for faster iterative builds
recommendations.rationale: With KAPT and Hilt, incremental builds benefit significantly from caching strategies

recommendations.recommendation: Version pin testing dependencies and consider BOM for consistent transitive versions
recommendations.rationale: Multiple test library versions could lead to compatibility issues; BOM approach simplifies management
